classDescription: A full caster who combines healing, radiant control, and Channel Divinity — with a domain subclass that fundamentally shapes their spells, armor, and combat identity from level 1.
roleIdentity: Durable support caster with healing, buffs, and radiant control.

cleric-spellcasting.summary: Prepare spells from the full Cleric list each day using Wisdom as your spellcasting ability.
cleric-spellcasting.description: You are a full spellcaster who prepares divine spells from the entire Cleric spell list after each Long Rest. The number of spells you can have prepared equals your Wisdom modifier plus your Cleric level (minimum 1). You use Wisdom for spell attack rolls and your spell save DC (8 + proficiency bonus + Wisdom modifier). You can cast any prepared spell using an appropriate spell slot, and you can cast Cleric spells as rituals if they have the ritual tag, without expending a spell slot. You know 3 cantrips at level 1, gaining more at levels 4 and 10.

cleric-divine-order.summary: Choose Protector (Heavy Armor and Martial weapon proficiency) or Thaumaturge (extra cantrip and Wisdom bonus to Arcana and Religion).
cleric-divine-order.description:
At level 1, choose one of two Divine Orders that shapes your clerical training:

Protector: You gain proficiency with Heavy Armor and Martial weapons, letting you fight on the front line alongside your allies.

Thaumaturge: You learn one additional Cleric cantrip of your choice, and you add your Wisdom modifier to any Intelligence (Arcana) or Intelligence (Religion) check you make.

choices.name: Protector Order
choices.description: You gain proficiency with Heavy Armor and Martial weapons. This allows you to wear plate armor and wield martial weapons such as longswords and war picks without penalty.

choices.name: Thaumaturge Order
choices.description: You learn one additional Cleric cantrip of your choice. Additionally, you add your Wisdom modifier to any Intelligence (Arcana) or Intelligence (Religion) ability check you make.

cleric-channel-divinity-1-use.summary: Channel divine power to fuel Divine Spark, Turn Undead, and your domain's Channel Divinity options.
cleric-channel-divinity-1-use.description: Starting at level 2, you can channel divine energy directly from your deity to fuel magical effects. You start with 1 Channel Divinity use per rest, gaining 2 uses at level 6 and 3 uses at level 18. Each Channel Divinity use powers one of the following actions: Divine Spark, Turn Undead, or a special option granted by your Divine Domain subclass. Spending a use is part of the action that triggers the effect.
cleric-channel-divinity-1-use.recovery: Regain all expended uses on a Short or Long Rest.

cleric-divine-spark.summary: As a Magic action, spend a Channel Divinity use to roll 1d8 + Wisdom modifier and restore HP or deal Necrotic damage to one creature within 30 feet.
cleric-divine-spark.description:
As a Magic action, spend one Channel Divinity use and point your holy symbol at one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. You roll 1d8 and add your Wisdom modifier to the result, then choose one of the following effects:

Positive Energy (Healing): The target regains hit points equal to the roll. No saving throw — the healing simply occurs.

Negative Energy (Necrotic Damage): The target takes Necrotic damage equal to the roll. The target makes a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC, taking half damage on a success.

You can spend additional Channel Divinity uses (up to your maximum) as part of the same Magic action to increase the roll — add 1d8 per extra use spent.
cleric-divine-spark.effect: Roll 1d8 + Wisdom modifier; restore that much HP or deal that much Necrotic damage (Con save for half) to one creature within 30 feet.
cleric-divine-spark.usage: Spend 1 or more Channel Divinity uses (Magic action)
cleric-divine-spark.recovery: Channel Divinity refreshes on a Short or Long Rest.

cleric-turn-undead.summary: As a Magic action, spend a Channel Divinity use to force every Undead within 30 feet to make a Wisdom save or be Turned for 1 minute.
cleric-turn-undead.description:
As a Magic action, spend one Channel Divinity use and present your holy symbol. Each Undead creature within 30 feet of you that can see or hear you must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC.

On a failed save, the creature is Turned for 1 minute. A Turned creature must spend its turns trying to move as far away from you as it can, and it can't willingly move to a space within 30 feet of you. It can only take the Dash, Disengage, or Dodge action on its turn, and it can't take Reactions. The Turned condition ends early if the creature takes any damage.

At level 5, any Undead that fails this save and has a Challenge Rating at or below half your proficiency bonus is instantly destroyed (see Smite Undead).
cleric-turn-undead.effect: On a failed save, the Undead is Turned for 1 minute: must flee, can only Dash/Disengage/Dodge, can't take Reactions. Turn ends if the creature takes damage.
cleric-turn-undead.recovery: Channel Divinity refreshes on a Short or Long Rest.

cleric-smite-undead.summary: When you use Turn Undead, Undead with a CR at or below half your proficiency bonus are instantly destroyed if they fail the save.
cleric-smite-undead.description:
Starting at level 5, your Turn Undead gains a lethal edge against weaker undead. When you use Turn Undead and an Undead creature fails its Wisdom saving throw, if that creature's Challenge Rating is equal to or less than half your proficiency bonus (rounded down), it is instantly destroyed rather than merely Turned.

At level 5, your proficiency bonus is +3, so Undead with CR 1 or lower are destroyed. At level 9 (proficiency bonus +4), CR 2 or lower. At level 13 (proficiency bonus +5), CR 2 or lower. At level 17 (proficiency bonus +6), CR 3 or lower.

cleric-divine-domain-subclass.summary: Choose your Divine Domain at level 3, gaining domain spells always prepared plus unique Channel Divinity options and features at levels 3, 6, 17.
cleric-divine-domain-subclass.description:
At level 3, you choose a Divine Domain that represents your deity's sphere of influence — such as Life, Light, Trickery, War, Knowledge, or Nature. This choice is your subclass gate: it permanently shifts the identity of your cleric, shaping your prepared spells, Channel Divinity, and subclass features at levels 3, 6, and 17.

Domain Spells: A list of spells that are always prepared for you and don't count against your number of prepared spells. These appear on your sheet automatically.

Channel Divinity Option: A unique Channel Divinity use available only to clerics of your domain (in addition to Divine Spark and Turn Undead).

Subclass Features: Additional class features at levels 3, 6, and 17 that reinforce your domain's combat, support, or utility identity.

cleric-blessed-strikes.summary: Choose Potent Spellcasting (add Wisdom modifier to Cleric cantrip damage once per turn) or a domain-granted weapon damage rider.
cleric-blessed-strikes.description:
At level 8, you gain one of the following options (some domains grant a specific version automatically):

Potent Spellcasting: When you deal damage with a Cleric cantrip, you can add your Wisdom modifier to the damage roll once per turn. This applies to cantrips such as Sacred Flame, Toll the Dead, and Word of Radiance.

Divine Strike (granted by some domains): Once per turn when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 damage of a type determined by your domain (such as Radiant for Life or Psychic for Knowledge). This increases to 2d8 at level 14.

cleric-divine-intervention.summary: As a Magic action, implore your deity for aid and roll a d20 — on 11 or higher, your god intervenes. Once per Long Rest.
cleric-divine-intervention.description:
As a Magic action, you describe the type of divine aid you desire and roll a d20. On a 1–10, your plea fails and nothing happens. On an 11–20, your deity intervenes: the DM chooses the form of intervention, which might duplicate any Cleric spell or Channel Divinity option, or take a form the DM decides is appropriate to your deity.

If your god intervenes, you can't use Divine Intervention again until you finish a Long Rest. If it doesn't intervene, you can try again the next time you take a Magic action to use this feature.

At level 20, this feature is replaced by Greater Divine Intervention, which automatically succeeds.
cleric-divine-intervention.usage: Magic action; roll d20 — intervention occurs on 11–20
cleric-divine-intervention.recovery: Once per Long Rest if the intervention occurs. No restriction if it fails.

cleric-greater-divine-intervention.summary: Divine Intervention automatically succeeds. You can cast Holy Aura or Wish without a spell slot as part of the effect. Once per Long Rest.
cleric-greater-divine-intervention.description:
At level 20, Divine Intervention is replaced by Greater Divine Intervention. When you use this feature, it automatically succeeds — no d20 roll required. Your deity answers your call and intervenes in a dramatic fashion.

As part of the intervention, you can cast Holy Aura or Wish without expending a spell slot. The DM may also choose a form of intervention appropriate to your deity in addition to or instead of the spell.

Once you use Greater Divine Intervention, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

cleric-spell-level-2.summary: Unlock 2nd-level spell slots, expanding your prepared list into stronger healing, control, and offensive options.
cleric-spell-level-2.description: 2nd-level spells open access to powerful cleric options: Spiritual Weapon, Hold Person, Silence, Aid, and Enhance Ability. Your prepared list grows with your level, so higher-tier spells become available without giving up access to 1st-level slots.

cleric-spell-level-3.summary: Unlock 3rd-level spell slots — a significant jump in available spell power and battlefield control options.
cleric-spell-level-3.description: 3rd-level spells are a major spike for the cleric: Dispel Magic, Revivify, Spirit Guardians, Mass Healing Word, and Bestow Curse are all available here. Proficiency bonus also increases to +3 at this level.

cleric-spell-level-4.summary: Unlock 4th-level spell slots and expand access to stronger domain expressions and defensive options.
cleric-spell-level-4.description: 4th-level spells include Banishment, Death Ward, Freedom of Movement, Guardian of Faith, and Stone Shape. Your expanded prepared list can now address a wider variety of threats.

cleric-spell-level-5.summary: Unlock 5th-level spell slots — the tier where cleric spellcasting reaches its most powerful general-purpose options.
cleric-spell-level-5.description: 5th-level spells are a turning point: Flame Strike, Mass Cure Wounds, Raise Dead, Scrying, and Commune are all available. Proficiency bonus increases to +4. This is often the level where the cleric's support and control output feels definitively powerful.

cleric-spell-level-6.summary: Unlock 6th-level spell slots, extending into rare divine magic with powerful control and recovery effects.
cleric-spell-level-6.description: 6th-level spells open Heal, Harm, Blade Barrier, Find the Path, and True Seeing. At this tier, single spell slots can swing an entire encounter.

cleric-spell-level-7.summary: Unlock 7th-level spell slots and access to some of the most powerful divine magic available.
cleric-spell-level-7.description: 7th-level spells include Conjure Celestial, Divine Word, Plane Shift, and Resurrection. Proficiency bonus increases to +5.

cleric-spell-level-8.summary: Unlock 8th-level spell slots and a near-pinnacle tier of divine power.
cleric-spell-level-8.description: 8th-level spells include Antimagic Field, Control Weather, Earthquake, and Holy Aura. These spells can reshape the battlefield entirely.

cleric-spell-level-9.summary: Unlock 9th-level spell slots — the highest tier of spellcasting, available to very few beings in the world.
cleric-spell-level-9.description: 9th-level spells include True Resurrection, Mass Heal, Gate, and Wish (if on the list). Proficiency bonus increases to +6. A single 9th-level spell can fundamentally alter the course of a campaign moment.

cleric-channel-divinity-2-uses.summary: Channel Divinity now refreshes to 2 uses per rest, giving you significantly more resource flexibility.
cleric-channel-divinity-2-uses.description: With 2 Channel Divinity uses, you can spend one early in a fight and still have a second for the domain option or Turn Undead when the moment demands it. Recovery still happens on a short or long rest.
cleric-channel-divinity-2-uses.recovery: Regain all expended uses on a Short or Long Rest.

cleric-channel-divinity-3-uses.summary: Channel Divinity now provides 3 uses per rest — enough to be a consistent tactical tool across every fight.
cleric-channel-divinity-3-uses.description: With 3 Channel Divinity uses per rest, the high-level cleric can treat this resource as a reliable part of every encounter, not just a once-per-fight emergency button. Domain Channel Divinity options become much more deployable.
cleric-channel-divinity-3-uses.recovery: Regain all expended uses on a Short or Long Rest.

cleric-cantrips-known-4.summary: You now know 4 cleric cantrips, giving you a broader zero-slot toolkit.
cleric-cantrips-known-4.description: A fourth cantrip slot lets you round out your cantrip coverage: typically one offensive option (Sacred Flame or Word of Radiance), one utility (Guidance, Light, Thaumaturgy), and one situational pick.

cleric-cantrips-known-5.summary: You now know 5 cleric cantrips — a full set covering offense, utility, and defense.
cleric-cantrips-known-5.description: 5 cantrips is the Cleric's maximum. By level 10, this should represent a well-rounded set that covers all major zero-slot scenarios your build needs.

cleric-ability-score-improvement-4.summary: Improve two ability scores by 1 each, or take a feat to add a specialized capability.
cleric-ability-score-improvement-4.description: Most Cleric builds increase Wisdom first (to improve spell save DC, healing rolls, and spell attacks) or Constitution (to improve HP and Concentration saves). Feats like War Caster or Resilient (Constitution) are strong options for Concentration-dependent builds.

cleric-ability-score-improvement-8.summary: Second ASI or feat — continue improving Wisdom or Constitution, or add a feat that suits your domain identity.
cleric-ability-score-improvement-8.description: By level 8, consider whether your primary stats are high enough or if a feat adds more value. Spell Sniper, Inspiring Leader, or additional saving throw proficiency can all be strong picks depending on your domain and party role.

cleric-ability-score-improvement-12.summary: Third ASI or feat — shore up weaknesses or maximize your most impactful stat.
cleric-ability-score-improvement-12.description: If Wisdom or Constitution are not yet at their target scores, this is the level to close the gap. Alternatively, a utility feat can add out-of-combat flexibility or defense.

cleric-ability-score-improvement-16.summary: Fourth ASI or feat — high-level refinement of your cleric build.
cleric-ability-score-improvement-16.description: By level 16, most primary stats are maximized. This is a natural point for a feat that rewards the build you have been running rather than the one you planned at level 1.

cleric-subclass-feature-6.summary: Your Divine Domain unlocks a second defining feature, deepening the domain's mechanical and thematic identity.
cleric-subclass-feature-6.description: The level 6 domain feature is a significant step in your domain's power curve. Life gains Blessed Healer, Light gains Improved Flare, Trickery gains Cloak of Shadows, War gains War God's Blessing. Each transforms the domain from an early-game identity into a sustained tactical asset.

cleric-subclass-feature-8.summary: Your Divine Domain grants a passive or active feature that reinforces its offense, defense, or support identity.
cleric-subclass-feature-8.description: The level 8 domain feature for most domains is Divine Strike (extra weapon damage) or Potent Spellcasting (extra cantrip damage). These features ensure the domain stays relevant in the baseline action economy between major spell turns.

cleric-subclass-feature-14.summary: Your domain grants its level 14 feature, and your Blessed Strikes bonus now applies twice per turn instead of once.
cleric-subclass-feature-14.description:
At level 14, two things happen simultaneously:

Improved Blessed Strikes: The benefit you chose for Blessed Strikes at level 8 now applies twice per turn instead of once. If you chose Potent Spellcasting, you can add your Wisdom modifier to Cleric cantrip damage twice per turn (on two different damage rolls or instances). If your domain granted Divine Strike, it now deals 2d8 extra damage per turn instead of 1d8.

Subclass Feature: Your Divine Domain also grants a feature at this level that further develops your domain's identity. The specific feature depends on your chosen domain.

cleric-subclass-feature-17.summary: Your Divine Domain unlocks its capstone feature, completing the domain's full mechanical identity.
cleric-subclass-feature-17.description: The level 17 domain feature is the final expression of the domain's identity. Life gains Supreme Healing, Light gains Corona of Light, Trickery gains Improved Duplicity, War gains Avatar of Battle. These capstones are significant power jumps that define how the domain plays at high levels.

cleric-epic-boon.summary: Choose an Epic Boon feat — a rare, powerful enhancement available only to characters at this level.
cleric-epic-boon.description: Epic Boons are very powerful feats gated behind high level play. They provide abilities beyond the normal scope of feat choices. Select the one that best matches your cleric's role and the campaign's remaining challenges.

progressionSummary.summary: Channel Divinity (1 use), Divine Spark, Turn Undead.
Ability Score Improvement, Subclass Feature, Blessed Strikes.

buildTips.primaryReason: powers your spells, healing, and save DC
buildTips.secondaryReason: improves survivability and Concentration on key buffs
buildTips.avoidDumpReason: even armored clerics benefit from initiative and Dexterity saves
